jobs.title: Developer
jobs.detail:
A Senior Developer is a highly experienced software professional who leads the technical direction of projects while mentoring junior team members.
They focus on high-level architecture, complex problem-solving, and ensuring the delivery of high-quality, scalable code.Core ResponsibilitiesTechnical Leadership:


Architecting complex software systems and making critical decisions on technology stacks and design patterns.Mentorship: Coaching junior and mid-level developers through code reviews, technical guidance, and professional development.Development: Writing advanced, clean, and secure code for business-critical systems.Project Oversight: Managing project timelines, translating business requirements into technical specifications, and collaborating with stakeholders.Quality Assurance: Establishing coding standards, implementing automated testing, and troubleshooting high-severity bugs.Key RequirementsExperience: Typically 5 to 10+ years of professional software development experience.Education: A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.Technical Proficiency: Mastery of multiple programming languages (e.g., Java, Python, C#, JavaScript) and deep knowledge of databases, cloud platforms, and DevOps.Soft Skills: Strong communication, strategic thinking, and the ability to work independently or lead cross-functional teams.Work Environment & Career PathWork Setting: Usually office-based or remote, working full-time with occasional overtime to meet major deadlines.Advancement: Senior Developers often progress into roles like Lead Developer, Principal Engineer, or Software Architect.Would you like to see a specific job description template or a list of common interview questions for this role?AI can make mistakes, so double-check
